content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/ Administration • • • Executive/ Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322 5660                322 5901              322 6441           322 6444

December 16, 1977

Phillip Cohen
Attorney-at-Law
266 Mobil Avenue, Suite 109
Camarillo, California 93010

Dear Mr. Cohen:

     Thank you for your letter of November 15, 1977, asking the
Commission's advice with respect to the question of whether the
Ventura-Santa Barbara Health Systems Agency is an "agency" within
the meaning of Government Code Section 82003, and hence required
to develop and promulgate a Conflict of Interest Code pursuant
to Government Code Section 87300, et. seq.

     We have recently received a similar request for advice
from the Health Systems Agency in Los Angeles County and have
numbered it Opinion Request No. 77-043. A copy of that request
is enclosed. The staff has determined to combine your request
for advice with that opinion request and attempt to resolve this
question in a manner which will have a uniform effect on all such
agencies in the State. Before determining the answer to your
question, we believe it necessary to obtain additional informa-
tion concerning the authority and functions of health Service
Agencies. This is necessitated in part by the fact that the
staff of the Commission has, on several previous occasions,
informed Health Systems Agencies that they were not subject to
the requirements of Section 87300. However, the questions your
letter and that of Ms. Grant raise may require us to reevaluate
that previously issued advice. I am enclosing copies of that
correspondence. I would invite your comments or the conclusion
and reasoning therein.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Kenneth W. Goshorn
                                        Research Specialist III
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

KWG:mfa
Enclosures

PHILIP COHEN
Attorney at Law
266 Mobil Ave., Suite 109
Camarillo, California 93010
(805) 484-3866

November 15, 1977

Fair Political Practices Commission
State of California
P.O. Box 807
1100 "K" Street Building
Sacramento, California 95814

Attn: Mr. Ken Goshorn

Re: Conflict of Interest
Gov. Code Secs. 87300 et. seq.

Dear Mr. Goshorn:

In accordance with our discussion earlier today, please
be advised that this office is legal counsel to the Ventura-Santa
Barbara Health Systems Agency. Our review of relevant legislation
pursuant to Conflicts of Interest, has resulted in the posing of
the following question.

Is the Ventura-Santa Barbara Health Systems Agency an
"Agency" (Gov. Code Section 82003) for which a Conflict of Interest Code (Gov. Code Sections 87300 et. seq.) must be promulgated
and submitted to the code reviewing body (Fair Political Practices
Commission) in accordance with Government Code, Sections 82011 and
87303).

On the one hand, it appears that the State of California
requires a Conflict of Interest Code drafted pursuant to the requirements of the Fair Political Practices Commission. On the
other hand, my discussion with you left me with the impression
that the Commission has not yet implemented a procedure relative
to entities such as the Ventura-Santa Barbara Health Systems Agency,
which is a non-profit corporation.

Although I am in the process of drafting a conflict of interest code for the Agency, and will of course submit it to the
Commission for appropriate action, I would very much appreciate
the response of the Commission to the question posed herein.

Very truly yours,

Philip Cohen
Attorney at Law

PC:bka

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Ventura-Santa Barbara Health Systems Agency, a non-profit corporation, qualifies as an "agency" under Government Code Section 82003 and is therefore required to promulgate and submit a Conflict of Interest Code to the FPPC.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request letter (dated November 15, 1977) from Cohen and the FPPC response letter (dated 1977, specific date unclear due to OCR). The response letter indicates this is correspondence acknowledging receipt of the request, stating staff will combine it with another similar request (Opinion Request No. 77-043) and requesting additional information before providing advice. OCR quality affects clarity of some dates and text portions.

embedding.summary: This document contains an incoming request from Philip Cohen, legal counsel to the Ventura-Santa Barbara Health Systems Agency, asking whether the Agency must adopt a Conflict of Interest Code under California Government Code Sections 87300 et seq. The FPPC response letter indicates the staff is combining this request with a similar opinion request (No. 77-043) and needs additional information before determining whether Health Systems Agencies are subject to these requirements, as prior staff advice had indicated they were not.
